11.reason: [Using `gets` is unsafe and has been removed from modern C standards; it may cause compilation errors with newer compilers. Use `fgets` instead.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 1.0 , reason:  [Using `gets` is unsafe and has been removed from modern C standards; it may cause compilation errors with newer compilers. Use `fgets` instead.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 1.0 , reason:  [Using `gets` is unsafe and deprecated; it may cause buffer overflow. More critically, in many modern environments, `gets` is removed from the standard library, leading to a compilation error.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 1.0 , reason:  [Using `gets` is unsafe and deprecated; it was removed in C11 and causes compilation errors with modern compilers]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 1.0 , reason:  [Using `gets` is unsafe and deprecated; it may cause buffer overflow. More critically, in many modern environments (like those with strict compilation flags), `gets` is not available, leading to a compilation error.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 1.0 , reason:  [Using `gets` is unsafe and deprecated; it was removed in C11 and causes compilation errors with modern compilers]
